aiDescription.summary: The "Retrieve Orders" export is a delta export that exports only changed data since the last run. It is not a one-to-many export and does not run in a sandbox environment. It is associated with the "orders" resource in version "v1" and the operation "orderlist_load_query". The export runs in debug mode until January 8, 2024, at 18:47:47 UTC. It uses an HTTP POST method with a relative URI of "/json.mvc" and expects a JSON response. It handles errors in JSON format as well. The export includes paging configuration and expects the response in the "data.data" path. It is associated with a REST adaptor and includes delta configuration with a date format of "X" and a lag offset of 2000 milliseconds.
aiDescription.detailed:
The export is named "Retrieve Orders" and it is of type "delta", which means it will export only the changed data since the last run. 

The export is not a one-to-many export, meaning it will not treat child records as main records for this step in the flow. 

The export is not configured to run in a sandbox environment. 

The export is associated with the "orders" resource in version "v1" and the operation "orderlist_load_query". 

The export is set to run in debug mode until January 8, 2024, at 18:47:47 UTC. 

The export uses an HTTP method of "POST" and the relative URI is "/json.mvc". The request body is a JSON object that includes various parameters and filters. 

The export expects a successful response in JSON format and handles errors in JSON format as well. 

The export uses a form-type of "http" and includes paging configuration. The paging method is set to "body" and the last-page status code is set to 404. The paging body is a JSON object that includes parameters for offset, store code, function, count, timestamp, and filters. 

The response from the export is expected to be in the "data.data" path. 

The export is associated with a REST adaptor and uses the same HTTP method, relative URI, and resource path as the HTTP configuration. 

The export includes delta configuration with a date format of "X" and a lag offset of 2000 milliseconds. 

The export also includes a raw data field, which contains a hash of the export configuration. 

Overall, this export retrieves orders from an application using a POST request with specific parameters and filters. It expects a JSON response and handles paging. It also includes delta configuration to export only the changed data since the last run.
